title: Seeding framework + production-isolation safety
architecture: host Go module -> direct Postgres (offset port) -> COPY/SQL; NOT ent-linking (app/internal/bootstrap is internal, unimportable) NOR CLI-shelling (the slow path); drift mitigated by M7b data-DNA (M7a-D3)

tests.gates: build + vet + -race + gofmt clean; shellcheck clean (migrate-demo.sh)

isolation_guard: 3 layers — CheckWrite (shared/external blocked on non-prod regardless of opt-in) + PreflightEnv (S3-public override, real-Clerk/Directus rejection) + AuditLog.AssertClean (zero-pollution proof). Registry: Directus/S3-public/Clerk/Customer.io/Brevo/AI=shared, coresignal=external, Postgres/Redis/pgvector=per-stack

live_proof.reproducible: yes — wipe -> migrate-demo.sh (applies global policy) -> stackseed -> 200, no manual SQL
live_proof.seed: org=1, identity=3, users=2000 (1000 users + 1000 memberships); isolation audit CLEAN
live_proof.arc: no token -> 'unknown viewer'; token pre-seed -> 403; token post-seed -> HTTP 200 {membershipsCount: 1001}
live_proof.vehicle: authenticated GraphQL membershipsCount query (the ent-wired path)

bugs_caught_by_proof: casbin g2 arg-order swapped (model matches g2(org,sub,role); row is (org,user,role)) — fixed in identity.go + regression test
migrate-demo.sh never bootstrapped the global Sentinel policy (init_policy.sql ~47 rows) — fixed (platform bootstrap, guarded fresh-only)
platform_findings: /api/workforce/* panics on a nil *sql.DB in the injected demo build (demo-build defect, not seeding) — noted for a future stack-build pass

decisions.M7a-D1: split M7 into M7a/M7b/M7c, all in v1.1 (user)
decisions.M7a-D2: the isolation guard is the load-bearing deliverable (hard tested boundary)
decisions.M7a-D3: perf path = direct Postgres COPY (internal-import blocked; CLI-shelling too slow)
decisions.M7a-D4: the live login->200 proof caught + fixed the g2 arg-order + the global-policy bootstrap
